# Quality of life
- Use history command to get feedback and then decide what alias to make

# my_config refactor (2026-07): decisions and deferred items

Recorded during the numbered-chain refactor. Notes for future work, not open
bugs unless marked.


## Latent bugs found during the refactor

Resolved in supplementary patches:
- `11_git_utils.sh`: the "usb-repos" skip read the never-assigned `repo_dir`
  (should be `repo_name`) in four functions -- stash_report, pull_all_repos,
  push_all_repos, prune_merged_branches -- so the skip never fired. Fixed.
- `11_git_utils.sh` prune_merged: `current_branch` was computed but unused; it is
  now an explicit guard alongside the existing `* ` marker skip. Fixed.
- `04_verify.sh`: the symlink-mismatch worktree heuristic read the undefined
  `MC_REPO_ROOT` (should be `MC_ROOT`), so every mismatch was demoted to a
  worktree warning and never counted as an error. Fixed.
- `00_config.sh`: `MC_REPOS_ROOT` was exported but never defined; removed from
  the export list. (Making the repos root a real, wired setting -- the git
  helpers currently hardcode `$HOME/personal_repos` -- remains a possible
  enhancement.)
- `01_activate.sh`: `VISUAL` was set from the leaked editor-loop variable rather
  than the chosen `EDITOR`; now set from `$EDITOR`. Fixed.

Still open:
- `01_activate.sh`: lesspipe is initialized via `eval` (standard idiom, fixed
  command; intentionally left as-is).

## Deferred: MC_PS1 non-idempotency (external usb-sh)
On re-source (`mc_reload`) the USB prompt indicator is lost. `00_config.sh` sets
`MC_PS1` to a constant on each source, and `usb-sh/usb.sh` (sourced by
`06_usb.sh`) prepends the indicator under a one-time guard that does not
re-apply after the reset. The my_config side is idempotent; the fix belongs in
usb-sh (re-apply when the prefix is absent, or drive the indicator from
`PROMPT_COMMAND`).

## Decisions made during the refactor
- History variables: `01_activate.sh` now reads the canonical `MC_HIST_*` names.
  Previously it read `MC_HISTCONTROL`/`MC_HISTSIZE`/`MC_HISTFILESIZE`, which were
  undefined and exported empty (behavior change).
- `MC_COLOR_SUPPORT` was removed: dead repo-wide (no consumer).
- `_is_inside_git_repo` is duplicated in `10_vim_utils.sh` and `11_git_utils.sh`
  rather than shared, to avoid a cross-file load-order dependency. No shared
  abstraction was adopted yet.
- Two toolchain scripts (`miniforge_setup.sh`, `rsync_git_repositories.sh`) are
  excluded from the lint shellcheck pass via a named skip-list (conda-managed /
  flagged for review). Alternative: in-place shebang + `[[ && ]]` fixes.
- The message engine was extracted to `lib/message.sh` (VERSION 1), with
  `03_message.sh` reduced to a thin shim.
- Settings use a `##` documentation convention parsed by
  `lib/config_inspect.awk` and surfaced by `mc_config`. This requires bash 4.3+
  (namerefs). The command/parser names were reconstructed during the refactor.
- `setup_symlinks.sh` was retired in favor of `bootstrap.sh`, which creates the
  symlinks declared in `MC_SYMLINKS`; the `vimrc.vim` link was migrated into
  `MC_SYMLINKS`.
- The double-source idempotency check ignores volatile terminal/timer variables
  (COLUMNS, LINES, EPOCHSECONDS, ...) so only genuine drift is reported.
- The one-time `jobmigrate` migration helper (the 2026-06-23 kbd
  relocation) was removed from `15_job_hunt.sh` now that the relocation
  is complete on the owner's machine (C15). The dated design record
  `docs/260623_job-hunt_kbd-migration.md` is deliberately retained as
  history and left unmodified.

## Possible follow-ups
- Colors: consolidated into `lib/message.sh` (removed from `00_config.sh`). If
  colors are later needed independently of the message engine, consider a
  standalone `lib/colors.sh` that both can source.
- Reconsider whether `vimrc.vim` (`~/.vimrc`) is still wanted now that nvim is
  primary.

## Tier 2 test hardening (C19-C23): coverage and findings

Characterization/regression tests added as a safety net before the planned
`11_git_utils.sh` / `15_job_hunt.sh` review. Tests adapt to current behavior;
nothing below changed runtime code. Items marked "characterization" pin current
behavior and are load-bearing -- revisit them when behavior changes on purpose.

Coverage added (`bash/test/`):
- `git_repo_loops.sh` -- stash_report, pull_all_repos, push_all_repos,
  prune_merged_branches, and the usb-repos skip regression, against real local
  git fixtures (`fixtures/git_fixtures.sh`).
- `git_worktrees.sh` -- new_worktree (happy path plus failure paths) and
  rebase_worktrees_on_main.
- `friction_awk.sh` -- golden-file tests for
  `scripts/mc_friction_process_entries.awk` (show no-filter/exact/prefix,
  stale_before, archive with project+before_date, malformed), with
  `fixtures/friction/` inputs and committed expected outputs.

Deliberate non-coverage:
- Network/remote edges of pull/push (auth, offline remotes, non-ff or diverged
  conflict resolution): fixtures use on-disk bare remotes only.
- tmux integration: the current worktree functions invoke no tmux, so there is
  nothing to integrate against.
- The friction awk stderr summary formatting (percentages, histogram bars): an
  unspecified implementation detail, asserted only by targeted counts.

Observed behavior worth revisiting (characterization):
- `11_git_utils.sh` push_all_repos: the "early exit if nothing to push" block
  has no `return`; the guard arithmetic is discarded and control falls through
  to the empty push loop. The final return is coincidentally equivalent, but
  "Found 0 repo(s)" / "Complete: 0/0" still print.
- usb-repos is skipped by a silent `continue` in all four loop functions -- no
  skip-notice is emitted. Tests assert the untouched state; the notice check is
  a SKIP.
- pull_all_repos auto-stashes only tracked changes (`git diff-index --quiet
  HEAD`); an untracked-only dirty tree is not stashed, and a stash that
  conflicts with the pulled commit would leave the pop unresolved (unasserted).
- new_worktree requires a pre-existing branch; it does not create one. The
  failure path is an existing destination or a missing branch.
- rebase_worktrees_on_main ignores its documented `[worktree-root]` argument;
  worktrees are discovered via `git worktree list` relative to the current
  directory.
- friction awk: malformed detection is format-only -- an impossible but
  well-formed date (e.g. 2026-13-99) is not flagged; only a missing project tag
  is. `before_date` is exclusive (`entry_date >= before_date` is excluded). The
  documented `-v today=` variable is accepted but unused.

Test-infra notes:
- `scripts/lint.sh` does not scan `bash/test/` (its glob is `bash/*.sh
  dotfiles/bashrc.sh scripts/*.sh lib/*.sh bootstrap.sh`), so test files are not
  linted by the batch gate; they are kept shellcheck-clean by hand. A
  pre-existing SC1090 in `config_and_message.sh` was never surfaced for this
  reason (annotated in C19).
- `config_and_message.sh` was made self-locating from `BASH_SOURCE` (C19); it
  still carries a duplicate `#!/bin/bash` on line 2 (inert; left as-is).
- `bash/test/extension_system.sh` (pre-existing, out of tier scope) writes to
  the real `~/.config` and sources `~/.bashrc`, contrary to the sandbox rule the
  new tests follow: HOME is redirected into a mktemp sandbox, no real HOME /
  global git config / MC_REPOS_ROOT is touched, and git is forced noninteractive
  (`GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, `GIT_EDITOR`/`GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR=true`).
- `13_friction.sh` creates `MC_FRICTION_DIRECTORY` and its `archive` via
  `mkdir -p` at source time (default path is under `MC_REPOS_ROOT`), a
  filesystem side effect on sourcing; the friction tests invoke the awk backend
  directly to avoid it. Future cleanup: lazy-create on first use.
